concentration.headline: Highly distributed urban network
concentration.body: No single city overwhelms Germany's urban landscape. Berlin holds 6.9% of tracked urban population across 1758 cities — characteristic of federal or historically polycentric countries with multiple strong economic centers.
concentration.comparison: Similar to: United States, Germany, Switzerland

gravity.headline: Dense gravitational cluster — Cologne & Düsseldorf
gravity.body: The strongest gravitational pull exists between Cologne and Düsseldorf (34 km apart, combined population 1,718,570). Cities form a tight gravitational cluster with top pairs averaging 69 km apart. This dense sub-regional network often functions as a single megalopolitan economy.

narration.synthesis: Germany presents a classic polycentric structure: no primate city, with Berlin holding just 6.9% of tracked urban population. Gravity is strongest within a tight sub-regional cluster (Cologne → Düsseldorf, 69 km average separation) rather than radiating from a single capital — typical of federal economies with distributed industrial heritage.
